$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `prompt_templates` repo (Phase-2, repo
drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness prompt_templates tier-2 test).
What this repo adds: the first JSON ARRAY column (`tags` -> compact JSON
text), several nullable string columns (`userId` null-for-built-in,
`update`/`delete` refuse to mutate a row with `isBuiltIn=true`, returning a
not-modified result (v4: null / false) and leaving the row untouched.
The guard is exercised two ways, each flagged `expectNoop: true`: an update
and a delete that both target the built-in seed row. Both sides assert the op
reported not-modified, AND the final dump must match (the built-in row stays
byte-identical; a port missing the guard would have changed/removed it).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the other
fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.name: My Companion
seed.content: You are a warm, attentive companion.

seed.name: Sample ROMANTIC
seed.content: You are a romantic lead in a sweeping tale.
seed.description: ROMANTIC prompt optimized for CLAUDE models

ops.comment: New user template. Exercises the JSON array column (one tag), a NULL description, and non-null category/modelHint; id + timestamps pinned.

data.name: Coding Helper
data.content: You are a meticulous pair programmer.

ops.comment: Update the user template `My Companion`: new description + REPLACE the tags array (two tags -> one different tag). updatedAt pinned, createdAt preserved.

data.description: A friendly companion, now with wit

ops.comment: GUARD: attempt to rename the BUILT-IN seed row. Must be a no-op (v4 returns null); the row stays byte-identical.
GUARD: attempt to delete the BUILT-IN seed row. Must be a no-op (v4 returns false); the row stays.
Delete the user template `My Companion` (not built-in) — succeeds.
